mistralai/Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2.matched_phenotypes: telangiectasia, cafe-aux-lait spots, freckling, neurofibromas, optic gliomas, Lisch nodules, short stature, pectorus excavatum, scoliosis, mental retardation

tall stature, long limbs, disproportionately long torso, pectus carinatum or exacvatum, scoliosis, joint laxity, ectopia lentis, mitral valve prolapse, dilation of the ascending aorta and aortic root, pulmonary complications

seizures, intellectual disability, epilepsy, adenoma sebaceum, shagreen patch, ash leaf spot, renal angiomyolipoma, hepatic rhabdoids tumors, retinitis pigmentosa, fibrous hamartomas of the brain, cardiac rhabdomyomas
coarse facies, corneal cloudiness, hearing loss, growth deficiency, heart defects, respiratory problems, abnormalities of bones and cartilages, herniated umbilicus, enlarged liver and spleen

proteinuria, acroparesthesias, angiokeratomas, gastrointestinal symptoms such as pain, bloating, and diarrhea, decreased sweating ability, corneal opacifications, cardiovascular involvement including arrhythmias and myocardial infarction
proteinuria, mental retardation, skeletal anomalies, characteristic odor, osteosclerosis, lens dislocation, Marfanoid body habitus, vascular damage leading to stroke, thromboembolisms
short stature, coarsening of face, thickened tongue with macroglossia, kyphoscoliosis, joint stiffness, genu varum, lumbar lordosis, delayed dentition, corneal opacity, conductive hearing impairment

dark urine from early childhood, progressive arthritis starting around age 10 years, brownish deposits called "ochronotic plaque" in connective tissue, particularly noticeable in ear canal, tendons, ligaments, intervertebral discs, and articular surfaces

coloboma, depigmented hair, bleeding tendency, normal platelets count but prolonged bleeding time, recurrent infections especially bacterial, granulomatous inflammation, albinism, sensorineural hearing loss, mildly reduced intelligence quotients
